summary: I was born and raised in Minnesota, and it has been my home my entire life. Growing up here, I experienced both the warmth of community and the challenges of racism, especially as a Hmong American. Minnesota is where I began my journey of political activism, starting with local campaigns and eventually becoming a delegate for Bernie Sanders. This place shaped my identity and my commitment to fighting for justice and equality.

In 2013, I had the opportunity to study abroad in Thailand, which was a meaningful experience for me. While I was there, I heard about the rising racism against Hmong communities back home, which made me feel guilty for not being present during those difficult times. This motivated me deeply to return and become an advocate for justice and equality for my people. Thailand was a turning point that strengthened my resolve to fight for my community.

My first real political involvement was with Dai Thao's campaign for reelection as a council member. I spent many hours campaigning for him because I believed he could uplift the Hmong community in Minnesota. Supporting Dai Thao helped me discover my passion for fighting for equality and justice. His campaign was a crucial step in my journey toward political activism and community leadership.

Leading up to the 2015 caucus, I participated in caucus training and was eventually elected as a Bernie Sanders national delegate from Minnesota. Attending the Democratic National Convention as his delegate was a life-changing experience that opened my eyes to the power of political engagement. Bernie Sanders' campaign inspired me to envision a future where I can speak out on issues of inclusion and justice for marginalized communities. Being part of his movement strengthened my commitment to public service and advocacy.

This past summer, I had the incredible opportunity to attend the Democratic National Convention as a Bernie Sanders national delegate. It was a transformative experience that made me realize the importance of having a voice in politics. Being there among so many passionate people motivated me to break through barriers of oppression and to one day run for office myself. The convention solidified my dedication to fighting for inclusion and equality for all marginalized groups.

I attended caucus training organized by Hmong community leaders in St. Paul, Minnesota, to prepare for the 2015 caucus. St. Paul is a central place for Hmong political organizing, and participating there helped me connect with others who share my passion for justice. It was in St. Paul that I took important steps toward becoming a delegate and deepening my involvement in local politics. This city has been a key backdrop for my growth as an activist and community advocate.
